title: riso-relettered-halo-rex-regvm
prompt:
Transform this engraving of a robed, crowned king into a TWO-COLOR RISOGRAPH print on warm off-white paper. Inks: FEDERAL BLUE for the king's line work, with a FLUORESCENT PINK halo disc behind him; where the two inks overlap they MULTIPLY into deep purple. Keep the crisp engraved cross-hatch lines of the original. Add visible riso character: slight registration offset between the blue and pink layers (pink shifted ~3px up-left), subtle paper grain, faint roller streaks.

Re-letter the circular halo: replace the old saint name with bold inscriptional Roman capitals reading "REX · REGVM · DOMINVS" arcing clockwise around the nimbus, epigraphic style with raised center-dot separators, matching the antique engraved letterforms. A small five-point star at the top of the arc. Keep the king's stern frontal face, spiked crown, and raised blessing hand exactly as in the source.

Devotional holy-card composition, centered, thin keyline border. 8.5x11 zine page feel.
